title: Bridge Essay: Visual mental imagery in typical imagers and in aphantasia: A millimeter-scale 7

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.

**Visual mental imagery in typical imagers and in aphantasia: Amillimeter-scale 7-T fMRI study.**. Cortex.. PMID: 40031090. DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2025.01.013. Most of us effortlessly describe visual objects, whether seen or remembered. Yet, around 4% of people report congenital aphantasia: they struggle to visualize objects despite being able to describe their visual appearance. What neural mechanisms create this disparity between subjective experience and objective performance? Aphantasia can provide novel insights into conscious processing and awareness.
